TechStackPage.title: Tech Stack

categoryDescriptions.languages: Core programming languages used in product delivery and frontend systems.
categoryDescriptions.frameworksLibraries: Framework and state patterns for scalable interfaces.
categoryDescriptions.database: Data layer technologies used for product persistence and analytics.
categoryDescriptions.styling: Design-system and styling approaches for maintainable UI.
categoryDescriptions.testing: Quality and confidence tooling for stable releases.
categoryDescriptions.tools: Execution environment and collaboration tools used daily.
categoryDescriptions.backend: Server and platform technologies used in full-stack delivery.

TechStackPage.methodDescription: I optimize for predictable delivery: clear architecture, reusable design systems, measurable performance, and collaboration-friendly code.

showcase.title: From newbie code to expert systems
showcase.description: Selected stacks where I can show how my implementation changes when the bar moves from “it works” to “it scales.”

react.summary: Replace manual loading state with React 19's built-in action model.
react.improvement: Moves from ad-hoc useState flags to useActionState — the form's action prop drives the async lifecycle, isPending replaces manual toggles, and success callbacks compose cleanly.

nextjs.summary: Use PageProps and async params to keep the route server-first with full type safety.
nextjs.improvement: Moves from a client-heavy page shell to an async Server Component typed with PageProps<'/route'> — the globally available helper generated by next typegen that resolves params as a Promise and eliminates manual type annotations.

typescript.summary: Use discriminated unions and satisfies to encode domain rules in the type layer.
typescript.improvement: Moves from a loose object literal to a discriminated union (kind: 'code' | 'narrative') verified with satisfies — TypeScript catches missing fields at the definition site, not at the call site.

playwright.summary: Structure tests as readable steps, not a flat sequence of awaits.
playwright.improvement: Moves from a single navigation to named test.step blocks — each assertion is scoped, failures point to the broken step, and the test report reads like a product spec.

supabase.summary: BaaS speed with full Postgres control — auth, real-time, and RLS in one platform.
supabase.improvement: Moves authorization out of application code and into Row Level Security policies at the database layer, reducing surface area for bugs and making access rules auditable alongside the schema.
supabase.detail: I reach for Supabase when I need to prototype at BaaS speed without giving up Postgres depth. Row Level Security encodes authorization rules inside the database itself — keeping API routes thin and access policies version-controlled. The real-time layer replaces polling with a WebSocket push channel, a natural fit for collaborative or live-updating surfaces. Supabase's auto-generated REST and typed client mean the schema becomes the contract, and breaking changes surface at build time rather than in production.

AboutPage.title: About Me
AboutPage.intro: I'm a passionate front-end developer with a focus on creating intuitive and engaging user experiences. My goal is to build products that are not only visually appealing but also highly functional and accessible.
AboutPage.aboutMeDescription: I'm a front-end developer with 3+ years of experience in building web applications using modern technologies like React. I have a strong understanding of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, and I'm always eager to learn new frameworks and libraries. My development philosophy centers around clean, maintainable code and a user-first approach. I believe that great design is not just about aesthetics but also about usability and accessibility. I strive to create products that are both beautiful and easy to use for everyone.
